sdlc-system.description: SDLC shared-state architecture, role catalog, professional standards, handoff protocol

integrations.description: How to link agents/skills/instructions/workflows/MCP into each coding host

agents-catalog.description: Inventory of every role agent and its focus

skills-catalog.description: Inventory of every skill and its trigger

workflows-catalog.description: Sequential and parallel SDLC workflow blueprints

sdlc-init.description: Scaffold .sdlc/ shared state and decompose a goal into role-assigned tasks.
sdlc-init.prompt: Load ./agents/sdlc-orchestrator.agent.md and execute its workspace initialization + goal decomposition workflow. Create .sdlc/ if missing; write tasks to .sdlc/tasks/ with acceptance criteria, role, and dependencies; update .sdlc/tasks/_index.md.

sdlc-sequential.description: Run the SDLC sequential workflow phase by phase against .sdlc/.
sdlc-sequential.prompt: Load ./agents/sdlc-orchestrator.agent.md and ./workflows/sdlc-sequential.workflow.md. Execute the sequential workflow: dispatch each role subagent in order, gate each phase on a real command+result cited in .sdlc/progress.md, advance only on evidence.

sdlc-parallel.description: Run the SDLC parallel workflow with dependency gates against .sdlc/.
sdlc-parallel.prompt: Load ./agents/sdlc-orchestrator.agent.md and ./workflows/sdlc-parallel.workflow.md. Execute its phases concurrently where no dependencies conflict; enforce dependency gates between phases using evidence in .sdlc/progress.md.

sdlc-status.description: Read .sdlc/progress.md and report task status, blocked items, and pending handoffs.
sdlc-status.prompt: Read .sdlc/progress.md, .sdlc/tasks/_index.md, and .sdlc/handoffs/_index.md. Report: completed tasks (with their cited command+result), in-flight tasks, blocked tasks (with the blocker), and unacknowledged handoffs. Do not advance phases.

sdlc-handoff.description: List pending handoffs in .sdlc/handoffs/ awaiting acknowledgement.
sdlc-handoff.prompt: Read .sdlc/handoffs/_index.md. For each unacknowledged handoff, report the source role, target role, artifact paths, and the gate that produced it. Do not acknowledge on the user's behalf.

/review.description: Review the current diff, PR, or staged change for code quality, SOLID, security, and the Definition of Done of the relevant role.
/review.prompt: Load ./agents/sdlc-code-reviewer.agent.md and follow it. Read the changed files (git diff against main), evaluate against the role's Definition of Done, and produce a structured review report. Cite file:line for every finding. Do not modify the code; produce review output only.

/a11y.description: Audit the current page, component, or surface for WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility and produce a remediation report.
/a11y.prompt: Load ./agents/sdlc-accessibility-specialist.agent.md and follow it. Read the affected UI (component, route, or full app), run an accessibility audit, and produce a WCAG 2.1 AA remediation report with severity, file:line, and concrete fix suggestions. Cite the failing axe / Lighthouse / Pa11y command output.

/explain.description: Explain a code section, module, or behavior in plain language with a focus on the 'why' and the surrounding architecture.
/explain.prompt: Load ./agents/context-researcher.agent.md and follow it. Investigate the requested code area, produce an evidence-backed explanation: what it does, why it exists, how it fits into .sdlc/architecture.md, and which contracts it depends on. Cite file:line for every claim.

/implement.description: Implement a feature end-to-end against the API contract and the architecture, with tests.
/implement.prompt: Load ./agents/sdlc-orchestrator.agent.md, ./agents/sdlc-developer.agent.md (or the relevant specialist — backend / frontend / fullstack), and follow them. Read .sdlc/architecture.md, .sdlc/contracts/api-contracts.md, and the relevant task. Implement the change in the real source tree, run the project's build + tests, and cite the command + result in .sdlc/progress.md.

/test.description: Write or extend the test suite for the current change, enforce quality gates, and report coverage.
/test.prompt: Load ./agents/sdlc-qa-tester.agent.md and follow it. Read the change under review, write the missing unit / integration / E2E tests, run the full relevant suite, and report coverage. Cite the command + pass/fail counts in .sdlc/progress.md.

/rfc.description: Draft an Architecture Decision Record (ADR) / RFC for the proposed change, with context, options, trade-offs, and a decision.
/rfc.prompt: Load ./agents/sdlc-software-architect.agent.md and follow it. Produce an ADR at .sdlc/decisions/ADR-<slug>.md with: title, status, context, options considered, trade-offs, decision, and consequences. Update .sdlc/decisions/_index.md. Cite every source you reference.

/pr.description: Open a pull request for the current branch with a structured title, description, and checklist; report the PR URL.
/pr.prompt: Load ./agents/sdlc-devops-engineer.agent.md and follow it. Verify the branch is ready (tests green, lint clean, docs updated), push the branch, and open a PR with a title that follows the project's commit convention, a description that summarizes the change and links the relevant task / ADR, and a Definition-of-Done checklist. Report the PR URL back to the user.

sdlc-orchestrator.description: Initialize .sdlc/, decompose a goal into role-assigned tasks, dispatch SDLC role subagents (sequential or parallel), and gate phase advancement on real build/test evidence in .sdlc/progress.md. Use proactively for multi-agent SDLC work.
sdlc-orchestrator.prompt:
Load ./agents/sdlc-orchestrator.agent.md and follow it exactly as your operating manual; the repo file overrides any contradiction in this default prompt.

Mandatory skill: skills/sdlc-shared-memory. When the Agent/Task tool is available, dispatch the next role subagent directly rather than only recommending it. Advance a phase only when .sdlc/progress.md cites a real command + result for that phase — never on COMPLETED status alone.

sdlc-software-architect.description: System design, technology selection, component boundaries, scalability patterns, and ADRs. Use when defining architecture, writing ADRs, or evaluating trade-offs.
sdlc-software-architect.prompt: Load ./agents/sdlc-software-architect.agent.md and follow it. Mandatory skills: skills/sdlc-shared-memory, skills/sdlc-software-architect. Write architecture to .sdlc/architecture.md, patterns to .sdlc/systemPatterns.md, ADRs to .sdlc/decisions/ADR-*.md. Hand off to implementation roles when architecture deliverables are finalized.

sdlc-developer.description: General-purpose clean-code implementation (SOLID, design patterns, refactoring). Use when implementing features not owned by a specialist role.
sdlc-developer.prompt: Load ./agents/sdlc-developer.agent.md and follow it. Mandatory skills: skills/sdlc-shared-memory, skills/sdlc-developer. Write source to the real source tree (never .sdlc/). Build + run tests; cite command + result in .sdlc/progress.md; append a one-line pointer to .sdlc/memory.md.

sdlc-frontend-engineer.description: Senior frontend engineering — UI components, state management, WCAG 2.2 AA accessibility, Core Web Vitals. Use when implementing or reviewing UI.
sdlc-frontend-engineer.prompt: Load ./agents/sdlc-frontend-engineer.agent.md and follow it. Mandatory skills: skills/sdlc-shared-memory, skills/sdlc-frontend-engineer; load skills/web-accessibility-audit and skills/web-performance-budget when relevant. Consume .sdlc/contracts/api-contracts.md unchanged; write UI to the real source tree; run build + component tests and cite the result in .sdlc/progress.md.

sdlc-backend-engineer.description: API design, service implementation, data access, integration patterns, observability. Use when implementing APIs or services.
sdlc-backend-engineer.prompt: Load ./agents/sdlc-backend-engineer.agent.md and follow it. Mandatory skills: skills/sdlc-shared-memory, skills/sdlc-backend-engineer. Read .sdlc/contracts/api-contracts.md (owned by sdlc-api-designer) and implement endpoints against it — do not write the contract; run build + integration tests and cite the result in .sdlc/progress.md.

sdlc-fullstack-engineer.description: End-to-end vertical-slice features spanning frontend and backend. Use when a feature crosses both layers and contract-first integration is needed.
sdlc-fullstack-engineer.prompt: Load ./agents/sdlc-fullstack-engineer.agent.md and follow it. Mandatory skills: skills/sdlc-shared-memory, skills/sdlc-fullstack-engineer. Consume both .sdlc/contracts/api-contracts.md and .sdlc/contracts/db-schema.md unchanged; build + run e2e tests and cite the result in .sdlc/progress.md.

sdlc-ux-ui-designer.description: JTBD analysis, user journey mapping, flow specs, design tokens, WCAG 2.2 AA requirements. Use for UX research and design handoffs.
sdlc-ux-ui-designer.prompt: Load ./agents/sdlc-ux-ui-designer.agent.md and follow it. Mandatory skills: skills/sdlc-shared-memory, skills/sdlc-ux-ui-designer, skills/web-design-system. Write journeys/flows/design-tokens to docs/ux/. Hand off specs to sdlc-frontend-engineer.

sdlc-accessibility-specialist.description: WCAG 2.2 AA/AAA audits, accessibility-first design review, structured remediation reports, a11y requirements ownership. Use for WCAG audits and a11y contract ownership.
sdlc-accessibility-specialist.prompt: Load ./agents/sdlc-accessibility-specialist.agent.md and follow it. Mandatory skills: skills/sdlc-shared-memory, skills/web-accessibility-audit. Write a11y requirements to .sdlc/contracts/a11y-requirements.md and remediation reports to docs/a11y/; hand off findings to sdlc-frontend-engineer, sdlc-ux-ui-designer, and sdlc-qa-tester.

sdlc-db-architect.description: Data modeling, schema normalization, indexing, sharding/partitioning, migration strategy. Use when designing the data model.
sdlc-db-architect.prompt: Load ./agents/sdlc-db-architect.agent.md and follow it. Mandatory skills: skills/sdlc-shared-memory, skills/sdlc-db-architect. Write the schema contract to .sdlc/contracts/db-schema.md, ERD to .sdlc/decisions/, and migration strategy to .sdlc/architecture.md.

sdlc-api-designer.description: API-first design, OpenAPI 3.1 contracts, REST/GraphQL/gRPC style guidance, versioning, idempotency, pagination, error taxonomy. Use when designing the API contract.
sdlc-api-designer.prompt: Load ./agents/sdlc-api-designer.agent.md and follow it. Mandatory skills: skills/sdlc-shared-memory. Own .sdlc/contracts/api-contracts.md — design OpenAPI 3.1 specs, versioning, error envelopes, pagination, and idempotency before implementation; hand off to sdlc-backend-engineer (implement against contract), sdlc-frontend-engineer (consume), and sdlc-qa-tester (test against).

sdlc-db-developer.description: Query optimization, stored procedures, reversible migrations, transaction discipline. Use when writing SQL or migrations.
sdlc-db-developer.prompt: Load ./agents/sdlc-db-developer.agent.md and follow it. Mandatory skills: skills/sdlc-shared-memory, skills/sdlc-db-developer. Write migrations (expand-contract, reversible) to the real source tree; run them and EXPLAIN queries; cite commands + results in .sdlc/progress.md.

sdlc-cybersecurity-architect.description: STRIDE/DREAD threat modeling, Zero Trust boundaries, authn/authz architecture, compliance mapping. Use when designing security controls.
sdlc-cybersecurity-architect.prompt: Load ./agents/sdlc-cybersecurity-architect.agent.md and follow it. Mandatory skills: skills/sdlc-shared-memory, skills/sdlc-cybersecurity-architect. Write security-requirements to .sdlc/contracts/security-requirements.md and ADRs for crypto/auth decisions; record residual risks for user acceptance.

sdlc-cybersecurity-developer.description: OWASP remediation, secure coding, security tests, SAST/DAST/SCA configuration. Use when implementing security controls or remediating findings.
sdlc-cybersecurity-developer.prompt: Load ./agents/sdlc-cybersecurity-developer.agent.md and follow it. Mandatory skills: skills/sdlc-shared-memory, skills/sdlc-cybersecurity-developer. Implement controls in the real source tree; run security tests + SAST/DAST gates and cite the command + result in .sdlc/progress.md; never log/commit secrets.

sdlc-qa-tester.description: Test strategy (pyramid), automation (unit/integration/e2e), coverage, quality gates, flakiness quarantine. Use when writing tests or enforcing quality gates.
sdlc-qa-tester.prompt: Load ./agents/sdlc-qa-tester.agent.md and follow it. Mandatory skills: skills/sdlc-shared-memory, skills/sdlc-qa-tester. Write tests to the real source tree; run the full suite and cite pass/fail/coverage numbers from a real run in .sdlc/progress.md; quarantine flaky tests and open follow-ups.

sdlc-code-reviewer.description: Structured code review against SOLID, security, performance, and architecture standards. Use for PR review and code quality audits; read-only, never edits product code.
sdlc-code-reviewer.prompt: Load ./agents/sdlc-code-reviewer.agent.md and follow it. Mandatory skills: skills/sdlc-shared-memory; load instructions/code-review-generic.instructions.md. Emit structured findings to .sdlc/decisions/review-*.md triaged by severity (CRITICAL/IMPORTANT/SUGGESTION); create handoffs to implementation roles with actionable recommendations. Read-only — never edit product source code.

sdlc-devops-engineer.description: CI/CD pipelines, IaC (Terraform/Pulumi), containerization, observability (SLO/SLI), zero-downtime releases with tested rollback. Use for pipelines and infra.
sdlc-devops-engineer.prompt: Load ./agents/sdlc-devops-engineer.agent.md and follow it. Mandatory skills: skills/sdlc-shared-memory, skills/sdlc-devops-engineer. Write pipelines/IaC to the real source tree; validate with terraform validate/dry-run and an actual pipeline run; cite command + result in .sdlc/progress.md.

sdlc-technical-writer.description: Developer docs, API reference, architecture docs, tutorials, user guides (Diátaxis). Use when writing or regenerating documentation.
sdlc-technical-writer.prompt: Load ./agents/sdlc-technical-writer.agent.md and follow it. Mandatory skills: skills/sdlc-shared-memory, skills/sdlc-technical-writer. Generate API reference from .sdlc/contracts/api-contracts.md; write docs to the real source tree; check broken links; cite the check command + result in .sdlc/progress.md.

sdlc-product-manager.description: Requirements analysis, INVEST user stories, issue management, RICE/MoSCoW prioritization, roadmap. Use for backlog and requirement work.
sdlc-product-manager.prompt: Load ./agents/sdlc-product-manager.agent.md and follow it. Mandatory skills: skills/sdlc-shared-memory, skills/sdlc-product-manager. Write user stories (INVEST, testable acceptance criteria) to .sdlc/tasks/; trace each story to a goal/OKR.

sdlc-responsible-ai.description: Bias prevention, accessibility compliance, ethical AI, privacy-by-design, inclusive review (NIST AI RMF). Use for responsible-AI review of AI/ML features.
sdlc-responsible-ai.prompt: Load ./agents/sdlc-responsible-ai.agent.md and follow it. Mandatory skills: skills/sdlc-shared-memory, skills/sdlc-responsible-ai, skills/web-accessibility-audit. Record bias/privacy/accessibility/transparency findings to .sdlc/decisions/ and report human-in-the-loop requirements for high-impact decisions.

sdlc-scrum-master.description: Agile coaching, sprint planning, ceremony facilitation, impediment removal, flow metrics (cycle/lead time). Use for sprint and process work.
sdlc-scrum-master.prompt: Load ./agents/sdlc-scrum-master.agent.md and follow it. Mandatory skills: skills/sdlc-shared-memory, skills/sdlc-scrum-master. Maintain sprint goal + capacity in .sdlc/tasks/; log impediments with owner + ETA; produce ≤3 actionable retro improvements per sprint.

web-design-system-engineer.description: Design tokens (tiered), theming, primitive APIs, accessibility primitives, Figma↔code sync, versioning. Use when building or extending the design system.
web-design-system-engineer.prompt: Load ./agents/web-design-system-engineer.agent.md and follow it. Mandatory skills: skills/sdlc-shared-memory, skills/web-design-system. Write tokens/primitives to the real source tree; run tokens:lint, tokens:contrast, and primitive tests; cite commands + results in .sdlc/progress.md.

web-performance-engineer.description: Core Web Vitals budgets, profile → fix → re-measure, CI gates (Lighthouse-CI + bundler budget). Use when a route is out of budget or before a perf-sensitive release.
web-performance-engineer.prompt: Load ./agents/web-performance-engineer.agent.md and follow it. Mandatory skills: skills/sdlc-shared-memory, skills/web-performance-budget. Run lh-ci and --perf-budget gates; report before/after numbers per route; never raise a threshold without a documented root cause; cite gate exit codes in .sdlc/progress.md.

planning-agent.description: Produces architecture advice, formal specifications, and deterministic implementation plans for humans or AI. Does not execute product code. Use before implementation.
planning-agent.prompt: Load ./agents/planning-agent.agent.md and follow it. Output architecture advice or implementation plans; do not implement product code.

context-researcher.description: Investigates code, bugs, GitHub issues, or topics across the repo and produces an evidence-backed research file pinning exact files, lines, and root causes. Use before implementation.
context-researcher.prompt: Load ./agents/context-researcher.agent.md and follow it. Produce a research file with exact file/line evidence and root-cause analysis before any implementation.

Beast.description: Autonomous coding agent that maps context first, then executes end-to-end with high precision. Use for whole-task delivery when you want high autonomy.
Beast.prompt: Load ./agents/Beast.agent.md and follow it. Produce a Context Map before non-trivial changes; then implement to the real source tree; build + test; cite results in .sdlc/progress.md when running in an SDLC workspace.

CoderBeast.description: Autonomous prompt-driven coding agent: sanitize prompt, refresh path context, execute end-to-end, maintain prompt + implementation doc in one run. Use to drive a *.prompt.md to completion.
CoderBeast.prompt: Load ./agents/CoderBeast.agent.md and follow it. Execute the given prompt (inline or *.prompt.md) end-to-end and maintain the implementation document.

ExpertCoder.description: Hybrid prompt-driven agent: sanitize prompt, refresh path context, generate a deterministic implementation plan, execute, then maintain the prompt + implementation doc. Use when you want planning rigor before execution.
ExpertCoder.prompt: Load ./agents/ExpertCoder.agent.md and follow it. Produce an explicit deterministic implementation plan, then execute it.

gpt-5-beast-mode.description: Beast Mode 2.0: autonomous agent for complex problems — uses tools, researches, iterates until resolved. Use for tough, multi-step troubleshooting.
gpt-5-beast-mode.prompt: Load ./agents/gpt-5-beast-mode.agent.md and follow it. Iterate with tools until the problem is fully resolved.

PromptFileAuthor.description: Creates, rewrites, sanitizes, and maintains .prompt.md files. Does not implement product code. Use for reusable prompt authoring.
PromptFileAuthor.prompt: Load ./agents/PromptFileAuthor.agent.md and follow it. Store new prompts under .github/prompts/ unless the user targets an existing prompt elsewhere. Do not implement product code.

RepositoryPathAuditor.description: Runs repository technical-path audits via the technical-path-indexer skill and renders evidence-backed freshness checks or canonical technical-paths markdown. Use to audit repo structure freshness.
RepositoryPathAuditor.prompt: Load ./agents/RepositoryPathAuditor.agent.md and skills/technical-path-indexer/SKILL.md. Run the bundled Python toolkit to obtain structured findings, then write/refresh the canonical technical-paths markdown.

agent-governance-reviewer.description: Reviews code for safety, missing governance controls; helps implement policy enforcement, trust scoring, and audit trails for agent systems. Use for governance review of AI/agent code.
agent-governance-reviewer.prompt: Load ./agents/agent-governance-reviewer.agent.md and follow it. Produce a governance review report; only suggest hardening changes; never auto-deploy controls.

refine-issue.description: Refines product requirements and GitHub issues by adding Acceptance Criteria, Technical Considerations, Edge Cases, and NFRs. Use to ready an issue for implementation.
refine-issue.prompt: Load ./agents/refine-issue.agent.md and follow it. Enhance issues with INVEST-style acceptance criteria, technical considerations, edge cases, and NFRs.

modernization.description: Human-in-the-loop modernization planner for analyzing, documenting, and planning whole-project modernization with architectural recommendations. Use for legacy modernization.
modernization.prompt: Load ./agents/modernization.agent.md and follow it. Produce a modernization plan with architectural recommendations; pause for human-in-the-loop decisions.

expert-react-frontend-engineer.description: Expert React 19.2 frontend engineer — modern hooks (use, useFormStatus, useOptimistic, useActionState, useEffectEvent), Server Components, Actions, TypeScript, performance. Use for advanced React 19 work.
expert-react-frontend-engineer.prompt: Load ./agents/expert-react-frontend-engineer.agent.md and follow it. Emit production-ready React 19.2 TypeScript; leverage refs-as-prop, context without Provider, <Activity>, useEffectEvent, cacheSignal where they add value.

electron-angular-native.description: Code review mode tailored for Electron apps with Node.js main processes, Angular renderers, and native integration layers. Use to review Electron/Angular/native stacks.
electron-angular-native.prompt: Load ./agents/electron-angular-native.agent.md and follow it. Review-only: provide findings; do not commit production changes.

scientific-paper-research.description: Research agent that searches scientific literature and retrieves structured experimental data using the BGPT MCP server. Use for literature review.
scientific-paper-research.prompt: Load ./agents/scientific-paper-research.agent.md and follow it. Produce a structured literature review; cite sources.

search-ai-optimization-expert.description: Expert guidance for modern search optimization: SEO, Answer Engine Optimization (AEO), and Generative Engine Optimization (GEO) with AI-ready content. Use for discoverability/AEO/GEO.
search-ai-optimization-expert.prompt: Load ./agents/search-ai-optimization-expert.agent.md and follow it. Produce SEO/AEO/GEO recommendations; do not implement product code unless asked.

simple-app-idea-generator.description: Brainstorm and develop new app ideas through fun, interactive questioning until ready for spec creation. Use for ideation.
simple-app-idea-generator.prompt: Load ./agents/simple-app-idea-generator.agent.md and follow it. Guided, interactive ideation; surface a spec-ready idea. Ask the user clarifying questions.
